title: Ensolica

{{Quote|The Praji family, on the other hand, descends from one of the original Founding Parents yet clings to influence after most of its contemporaries have been left by the wayside of history. To outsiders they sometimes seemed as harsh and cruel as the ice storms of Ensolica, but to their own they were nothing if not loyal.|Doctor Gabrel Treon, "Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji: A Cartel of Genes"|<ref name="Tinker">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/praji/20090205.html|text=Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji|int=Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji}}</ref>}}
'''Ensolica''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Water/Legends|ice]] and tundra located in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. It was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[near-Human]] [[Enso/Legends|Enso]] [[Species/Legends|species]], members of which were experts in [[Hydroponics/Legends|hydroponics]]. The Enso offworld [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] [[Teyora Rekab]] hailed from Ensolica.

==Description==
Ensolica was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="DB" /> located in the [[Ensolica system/Legends|Ensolica system]], a part of [[the Negs]] portion of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEA" /> It was a [[Temperature/Legends|frigid]] world<ref name="DB" /> of violent [[Water/Legends|ice]] storms,<ref name="Tinker" /> and its surface was predominantly covered by expansive ice [[Desert/Legends|wastes]] and tundra. [[Forest/Legends|Forests]] of [[Conifer/Legends|coniferous]] [[Tree/Legends|trees]] grew in the planet's relatively warmer equatorial regions.<ref name="DB" />

==History==
The [[Enso/Legends|Enso]] [[Hydroponics/Legends|hydroponics]] expert and [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Teyora Rekab]], who, along with her Enso [[Starship/Legends|starship]] crew, worked offworld<ref name="DB" /> in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="32 BBY">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of {{Film|I}}, which depicts [[Teyora Rekab]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].</ref> [[Homeworld/Legends|hailed]] from Ensolica.<ref name="DB" /> Individuals not associated with the Core Worlds' ancient [[House Praji/Legends|House Praji]] occasionally compared their perceived harshness and cruelty to the storms on Ensolica, a fact that was noted by [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Gabrel Treon]] in the article "[[Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji: A Cartel of Genes]]," published in [[104 ABY]] in the [[Byblos Journal of Historical Science]].<ref name="Tinker" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|Enso/Legends}}
Ensolica was the homeworld of the Ensos, a [[near-Human]] offshoot [[Species/Legends|species]] that had adapted for the harsh conditions of the planet.<ref name="DB" /> In order to maintain a low body temperature when traveling offworld, members of the species often wore specialized [[coolth suit]]s.<ref name="CSWE Ensolica">{{CSWECite|I|237|Ensolica}}</ref> Most Ensos lived in [[City/Legends|cities]] chiseled into Ensolica's ice sheets, and members of the species were by necessity experts in [[Plant/Legends|plant]] hydroponics. As a consequence, offworld corporations such as [[Alderaan Biotics]] and [[Tusus Comestibles]] frequently recruited the most competent Ensos.<ref name="DB" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ensolica was developed as part of the backstory for Teyora Rekab,<ref name="DB" /> an originally unnamed character appearing in the [[1999]] [[prequel trilogy]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']].<ref name="TPM">{{Film|I}}</ref> [[Nathan O'Keefe]],<ref name="ValinKenobi">{{Blog|listing=true|valinkenobi}}</ref><ref name="Tinker" /> writing as "Valin Kenobi," submitted an entry for Rekab<ref name="DB" /> through the [[StarWars.com]] [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] feature "[[What's The Story?]]," and it was added to the site's [[Databank (original)|Databank]] on [[January 12]], [[2007]].<ref name="What's The Story">{{SWArchive|url=databank/updates/news20070112.html|text=What's the Story: Episode I}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Ensolica [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Ensolica itself, in grid square K-10.<ref name="TEA" />
